howItWorks.intro: Intermittent fasting restricts when you eat, not what you eat. This calculator builds a personalized schedule with meal timing based on your chosen protocol.
rules: 16:8 is the most popular — fast 16 hours, eat within an 8-hour window
During the fasting window, only water, black coffee, and plain tea are allowed
Calorie targets are applied to the eating window — concentrate nutrition into fewer meals
howItWorks.outro: Research suggests intermittent fasting can improve insulin sensitivity, support fat loss, and simplify meal planning. Start with 16:8 before trying more aggressive protocols.

faq.question: Which intermittent fasting protocol is best for beginners?
faq.answer: 16:8 is the best starting protocol. Most people already fast 10-12 hours overnight, so extending to 16 hours means just skipping breakfast or having an early dinner. Once comfortable, you can try 18:6 or 20:4 if desired.

faq.question: What can I consume during the fasting window?
faq.answer: Water, black coffee, plain tea, and sparkling water are fine during fasting. Anything with calories, sweeteners (even artificial), or cream breaks the fast. Some purists also avoid flavored teas, but black/green/herbal tea is generally accepted.

faq.question: Will intermittent fasting cause muscle loss?
faq.answer: Not if you eat enough protein (0.7-1g per pound of body weight) and maintain a strength training routine. Research shows IF preserves muscle mass similarly to traditional dieting when protein intake is adequate.

faq.question: How long does it take to see results?
faq.answer: Most people notice reduced bloating and improved energy within the first week. Visible fat loss typically appears after 2-4 weeks of consistent fasting combined with a slight calorie deficit. Patience and consistency are key.

faq.question: Is intermittent fasting safe for everyone?
faq.answer: IF is not recommended for pregnant or breastfeeding women, people with a history of eating disorders, children under 18, or those with diabetes without medical supervision. Always consult a healthcare provider if you have a medical condition.

faq.question: What is the 5:2 method?
faq.answer: The 5:2 method involves eating normally for 5 days per week and restricting calories to 500-600 on the other 2 non-consecutive days. It's less about timing and more about weekly calorie reduction. Many people find it easier than daily fasting.

sections.title: What Is the Intermittent Fasting Calculator?
sections.body: The Intermittent Fasting Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes and fitness enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the fitness and training space. By entering your fasting protocol, eating window start time, goal, you get instant results including eating window, fasting window, estimated daily calories. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting eating window right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In fitness and training, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes and fitness enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your training with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your fasting protocol and need to find the right eating window. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Fasting Protocol
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your fasting protocol — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: eating window start time, goal, daily calories — optional. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your eating window, fasting window, estimated daily calories. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your fasting protocol before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both eating window and fasting window — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to optimize your training.
If you're unsure about your daily calories — optional, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
